meaning: adj.简洁的,紧身的,压缩在小范围内的
examples: The book gives an admirably succinct account of the technology and its history.
However, most of the writing is succinct; the historical accounts are clear and to the point; and the investigation of the causal links between liberalism and war is admirably thorough.
When Audrey Hepburn won the best actress for "Roman Holiday" in 1954, her speech was a perfect example of a succinct.
That may not be possible, observes political columnist Joe Klein, in a succinct piece.
You need to be succinct so people can understand this is where I stand.
